column1: # Generated supplemental for the German keyboard pack.
# Source: ß-fold restoration. The base words.tsv was imported from a Swiss
# German corpus (Switzerland abolished ß in 2006) and contains only ss-forms
# for words that take ß in modern standard German (DE-DE, AT-AT).
# Frequencies below are derived from the ss-form counts in words.tsv,
# discounted ~5% to acknowledge ambiguity (some ss-forms map to plain ss too).
# Entries follow the 1996/2006 spelling reform — pre-reform forms (beschluß,
# genuß, gewiß, daß, muß) are deliberately NOT included.
# weiß = white / knows (both fold to weiss in CH corpus)
# heißen — to be called / to mean
# süß family (s,ü,ß three-way diacritic test surface)
# Phone-vocabulary boost (merge-max): words present in words.tsv at very low
# counts because the Leipzig 2011 corpus is formal-text-skewed. These forms are
# extremely common in real phone typing.
# Iter E targets — boost above mid-frequency competitor false friends
# Iter K: boost targets that lost to shape-similar false friends on 1000w
# Iter L: boost ß forms above their ss counterparts and add nuanced false friends
# Boost lesser-used context-specific targets so they win over generic function words
# Device glide residuals: ordinary phone-domain words that were losing to
# shape-neighbour nouns or English forms on the Pixel replay.
